notes_md:
**làm gì** usually means “do what?” or “do anything,” depending on context. It’s used to ask about an action or to talk about whether an action happens.

As a question, it means “what to do / what are you doing?”  

In negative sentences, **không làm gì** means “not do anything” (literally “not do what”).  
It can also show annoyance or challenge in questions, like “why would (someone) do that?” depending on tone.

Close friend: **gì** = “what,” so think of it as “do what?” put together.

definitions.en: A phrase used to ask someone what they are doing.
definitions.es: Una frase utilizada para preguntar a alguien qué está haciendo.
definitions.vi: Câu hỏi dùng để hỏi ai đó đang làm gì.

translation.en: What do you want to do?

exampleSentencesTranslated: I'm free, if you want me to do anything just tell me.
She doesn't know what to do anything when facing a difficult problem.
If you don't want to do anything tonight, we can just relax.
notes:
làm gì usually means “do what?” or “do anything,” depending on context. It’s used to ask about an action or to talk about whether an action happens.

As a question, it means “what to do / what are you doing?”  

In negative sentences, không làm gì means “not do anything” (literally “not do what”).  
It can also show annoyance or challenge in questions, like “why would (someone) do that?” depending on tone.

Close friend: gì = “what,” so think of it as “do what?” put together.
exampleSentences: Anh đang rảnh, em muốn anh làm gì thì cứ nói nhé.
Chị ấy không biết nên làm gì khi gặp vấn đề khó khăn.
Nếu em không muốn làm gì tối nay thì chúng ta có thể nghỉ ngơi.

translation.en: What will you do this weekend?

content.sentence: Bạn sẽ ___ vào cuối tuần này.
